parsed_review.summary: CP00-657 (Risk C, 150 units, RP21.P06.M06.S04-RP21.P07.M03.S14) adds the Admin Console synthetic failure recovery bridge descriptor following the established descriptor-only pattern of CP645-CP656. Independent read-only inspection confirms the pack-level production_ready claim is supported: (1) descriptor and case set are strictly descriptor-only with every row descriptor_only:true/runtime_execution:false/raw_payload_included:false (service.js:3476-3611), and freezeCp657Result injects ~40 top-level false flags including executes_failure_recovery_runtime:false and persists_failure_receipt:false (service.js:593-640); (2) no-real-data/no-write boundaries are enforced in the bridge contract, test-evidence contract, and runtime boundary, with failure_recovery, permission_matrix, and Hermes runtimes all closed; (3) validateAdminConsoleCp657SyntheticFailureRecoveryBridgeDescriptor (validators.js:2317-2447) and validateAdminConsoleCp657Coverage rigorously check 38 false-flags, distribution (impl 40/security_audit 26/ui 17/claude_review 4/test 16/failure_recovery 44/hermes_evidence 2/fixture 1 = 150), RP21-only units, and handoff to CP00-658; (4) four CP657 tests assert these boundaries (model.test.js:939-999); (5) the contract projection sets current_pack=CP00-657, historical through CP656, validated by the contract script (validate-rp21-admin-console-contract.mjs:318-437); (6) command-evidence reports all 23 validation commands exit 0 with production_ready:false/no_real_data:true/writes_product_state:false. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic review packet/receipt handling (runtime_receipt_emitted:false, hermes_runtime_opened:false), not product runtime. Claude remains a read-only reviewer (read_only:true, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2:true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval:false) and is not final approver. Pending-state inspection/adjudication/claude-review-result artifacts correctly await this very review rather than prematurely asserting readiness. Review evidence is valid: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1442-byte well-formed JSON), inspection done via Read/Grep/Glob only, and this is the single review run with no prior raw-output/receipt. The pack does not claim runtime or enterprise trust, so the human-final-approval/enterprise-trust boundary remains correctly outside this receipt and does not block this CP closeout goal. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings.
